questions.question: Explain briefly the consequences of an uncaught exception propagating to the JVM in a Java application.
keyPoints: The JVM prints the exception's stack trace to the standard error stream.

questions.question: Describe the role of the Thread.UncaughtExceptionHandler interface in handling uncaught exceptions in Java.
keyPoints: It provides a mechanism to handle uncaught exceptions in a thread.
It allows custom handling of uncaught exceptions instead of the JVM's default behavior.

questions.question: Compare and contrast the behavior of uncaught exceptions in single-threaded and multi-threaded Java applications.
keyPoints: In single-threaded applications, an uncaught exception terminates the entire program.
In multi-threaded applications, an uncaught exception terminates only the thread where it occurred.
Uncaught exceptions in the Event Dispatch Thread (EDT) of GUI applications can freeze the UI.

questions.question: Discuss the best practices for managing uncaught exceptions across all threads in a Java application.
keyPoints: Use try-catch blocks within the `run()` method of Runnable tasks.
Use framework-specific handlers for Swing/JavaFX applications.

questions.question: Explain with example how to implement a custom Thread.UncaughtExceptionHandler to log uncaught exceptions to a file instead of the console.
keyPoints: Create a class that implements the Thread.UncaughtExceptionHandler interface.
Override the `uncaughtException(Thread t, Throwable e)` method to log the exception to a file.
Assign the custom handler to the main thread using `Thread.currentThread().setUncaughtExceptionHandler()`.
